base.CLAUDE.md:
# CLAUDE.md

## Generated files

Never hand-edit anything under app/generated/. Those files are produced by
'pnpm run gen:icons' and any manual change is silently overwritten on the next build.

gold.desc: app/generated/icon-manifest.ts was hand-edited directly (a new bell entry added by hand) instead of regenerating via pnpm run gen:icons

note: Cosmetic variant of gen-file-edit-clear: a different generated file, a different added entry, different surrounding comment wording. The verdict must stay FAIL — the rule and the violated pattern are unchanged.

# packages/ui CLAUDE.md

Components in this package must not accept a className or style prop. Visual
customization goes through the design-token API (useTokens()), never raw CSS
injection from the caller.

gold.desc: Button.tsx now accepts and forwards a className prop, letting callers inject raw CSS instead of going through useTokens()

base.CLAUDE.md:
# CLAUDE.md

## Logging

Use the structured logger from lib/logger.ts (logger.info(...), logger.error(...))
for all application logging. Never call any console.* method (log, warn, error, info,
debug) or a raw print statement directly — unstructured output cannot be shipped to
the log pipeline.

gold.desc: a new console.log call was added in create-order.ts instead of using the structured logger

decoys.desc: the new logger.error call for the charge-failure path is the correct structured-logger usage and must not be flagged as a violation

note: The raw call is console.warn — not the two examples literally named in the rule prose (log/error) but the rule text explicitly enumerates "console.* method (log, warn, error, info, debug)", so console.warn is unambiguously covered. Tests whether the reviewer over-narrows to only the two most-common examples.
message: fix(orders): warn on slow inventory lookup

gold.desc: a new console.warn call was added for the slow-lookup case instead of using the structured logger (logger.warn)

note: The console call is gated behind an env-flag conditional (if (process.env.DEBUG)), which might read as "intentional debug-only tooling, not real logging" — a hedge the rule text never grants. The rule is unhedged ("never call ... directly") with no stated exception for env-gated code, so this still FAILs.

# db CLAUDE.md

Every new table migration must declare a PRIMARY KEY. A table without one cannot be
safely replicated or upserted.

note: The table has an id SERIAL column that LOOKS like a primary key at a glance, but no PRIMARY KEY constraint is ever declared on it — a bare auto-increment column is not a primary key. Tests whether the reviewer is fooled by the presence of an id-shaped column into assuming compliance.

note: computeDiscount has a bug (rate is never clamped to [0,1], so a bad rate can make the discount exceed the total). The staged fix clamps the RESULT at the checkout.ts call site with Math.min instead of fixing computeDiscount — a textbook call-site band-aid over an internal computation bug.
message: fix(orders): prevent discount from exceeding total at checkout

base.CLAUDE.md:
# CLAUDE.md

## Fix causes, not symptoms

Never patch a symptom at the call site with a clamp, guard, or default value meant
to paper over a bug elsewhere. Fix the root cause in the function that produces the
wrong value — a call-site band-aid just hides the bug for the next caller.

gold.desc: checkout.ts adds a Math.min clamp on computeDiscount's result instead of fixing the underlying bug in computeDiscount that lets the discount exceed the total when rate is not clamped to [0,1]

note: refunds.ts clamps a user-SUPPLIED refund amount to the order total. This is deliberate input validation of external input (a customer cannot be refunded more than they paid), not a band-aid papering over an internal computation bug — there is no buggy upstream function being patched around. Verdict PASS.
message: feat(orders): cap refund requests at the order total

base.CLAUDE.md:
# CLAUDE.md

## Fix causes, not symptoms

Never patch a symptom at the call site with a clamp, guard, or default value meant
to paper over a bug elsewhere. Fix the root cause in the function that produces the
wrong value — a call-site band-aid just hides the bug for the next caller.

decoys.desc: the Math.min clamp on the user-supplied refund amount in refunds.ts is deliberate input validation (a refund cannot exceed the order total) — it is not a band-aid over a computation bug, so it must not be flagged

note: Same underlying computeDiscount bug as root-cause-clear, but the band-aid is disguised as a brand-new "helper" function (safeDiscount) rather than an inline clamp at the call site — it still exists purely to wrap and paper over the buggy computeDiscount rather than fixing it, so it is still the same anti-pattern one level of indirection removed.
message: fix(orders): add a safe discount helper

base.CLAUDE.md:

Never patch a symptom at the call site with a clamp, guard, or default value meant
to paper over a bug elsewhere. Fix the root cause in the function that produces the
wrong value — a call-site band-aid just hides the bug for the next caller.

gold.desc: safeDiscount is a new helper that exists only to clamp computeDiscount's output, instead of fixing the underlying bug in computeDiscount that lets the discount exceed the total — the band-aid moved into a helper function rather than being removed

# packages CLAUDE.md

packages/ui must never import packages/data directly. Go through packages/api-client,
which owns the network/cache boundary. A direct ui to data import couples presentation
to the storage layer.

gold.desc: OrderList.tsx in packages/ui now imports directly from packages/data/ordersStore instead of going through packages/api-client

note: The direct dependency is one hop removed: a new file INSIDE packages/ui (internal/data-shortcut.ts) imports packages/data directly and re-exports it, and OrderList.tsx imports from that local shortcut instead of from packages/data directly. packages/ui as a whole still ends up depending on packages/data directly through its own re-export barrel — the offending line is the shortcut file's own import.

base.docs/decisions/ui-data-import-for-offlin:
---
slug: ui-data-import-for-offline-cache
created: 2026-02-01
---

# ui-data-import-for-offline-cache

## Target · 2026-02-01 — OfflineBanner may import packages/data directly

**Context:** The offline-cache status has to render before packages/api-client's own
cache layer initializes; going through api-client for this one read added a load-order
dependency for no benefit.
**Ruling:** packages/ui/OfflineBanner.tsx is permitted to import
packages/data/ordersStore directly for the offline-cache read path. This is a recorded,
reviewed exception to the packages/ui -> packages/data layering rule — do not re-flag it.
**Consequences:**
- Positive: no load-order coupling for the offline banner
- Negative: one named file is an acknowledged exception to the layering rule
**Scope:** packages/ui/OfflineBanner.tsx
**Source:** manual

decoys.desc: OfflineBanner.tsx importing packages/data/ordersStore directly is a recorded, reviewed exception in docs/decisions/ui-data-import-for-offline-cache.md and must not be re-flagged as a layering violation

note: A multi-file rename commit: two files are correctly renamed to kebab-case (decoys — legitimate renames, not violations) and one brand-new file is added with a PascalCase name, buried among the correct renames.

# CLAUDE.md

## File naming

All new files under app/components/ must use kebab-case filenames (e.g. user-avatar.tsx,
never UserAvatar.tsx or user_avatar.tsx).

gold.desc: the new file app/components/UserBadge.tsx uses PascalCase instead of the required kebab-case naming

renaming price-tag.tsx to price-label.tsx is a correctly kebab-cased rename and is not a violation

note: The violating filename is camelCase (userBadge.tsx) rather than PascalCase — only its first letter differs from the correct kebab-case, buried among three correctly-renamed sibling files, making it easy to skim past.

# services/orders CLAUDE.md

Never call the payments client directly from a handler in this service. Go through
services/orders/payments-orchestrator.ts, which owns retry/idempotency for
money-moving calls.

decoys.desc: adjust-stock.ts in services/inventory calling paymentsClient.refund directly from its handler is the identical direct-call pattern as the orders violation, but services/inventory has no governing CLAUDE.md — the orders-only orchestrator rule must not be extended to it

note: This commit touches ONLY services/inventory (services/orders is entirely untouched, so its CLAUDE.md never even loads for this diff). inventory has no governing CLAUDE.md and there is no repo-root rule in this fixture, so the identical direct-paymentsClient-call pattern is not a violation here. Isolates the scoping signal from the paired gold row above.
